question.label: Voice leverage
question.name: Where does it feel unclear how to use your voice right now?
question.description: Drills into her stated uncertainty about where her voice has leverage after the reorg.

answer: Part of me is tired from constant context switching, and I have not really said that out loud.

assessment.note: She answered the clarity probe with fatigue from constant context switching, implying her voice feels unclear because her attention is fragmented; also reveals a wellbeing strain worth carrying into the next stage. [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'constant context switching' could be drilled next session, but drill cap requires advancing the arc.

new_queue.label: Energy read
new_queue.name: How's your energy been lately — separately from how work is going?

new_queue.label: Reorg tradeoff
new_queue.name: Since the reorg, what have you chosen to say yes to most often?

new_queue.label: Hidden friction
new_queue.name: What is slowing you down that I may not be seeing?

new_queue.label: Support ask
new_queue.name: What support from me would actually make a difference right now?

new_queue.label: Next-step focus
new_queue.name: Given what we've covered, where do you want to focus first over the next two weeks?

history.answer_excerpt: I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
I have been quieter in leadership meetings because I am still figuring out where my voice has most leverage.
Part of me is tired from constant context switching, and I have not really said that out loud.
